# 🇱🇰 This Week in Sri Lanka

*Generated by gpt-5 from 310 English News Articles published between 2026-06-12 and 2026-06-19.*

## 1. A US–Iran memorandum paused hostilities and signalled the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

Benchmark crude slid to <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">below $80 a barrel</span>, a three‑month low, trimming a surcharge that had raised import costs and pressured prices. <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">Equities in Colombo advanced</span> as risk premia eased, with turnover supported by crossings and broad gains across banking and capital‑goods counters.

The exchange rate steadied after weeks of volatility, while <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">bond yields eased</span> along the curve. Officials said shipping lanes would clear gradually as <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">mine‑removal and insurance reinstatement</span> progressed (the process by which underwriters resume cover after hostilities) over coming weeks.

## 2. The economy grew 5.1% in the first quarter of 2026.

Output at constant prices reached <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">Rs. 3.65 trillion</span>, up from Rs. 3.48 trillion a year earlier. <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">Industry expanded 7.2%</span>, led by a 16.3% surge in construction and a 19.5% rise in mining and quarrying.

<span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">Services rose 3.4%</span> with double‑digit gains in insurance and IT, while agriculture edged up 1.1% as tea and rubber weakened. At current prices, <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">gross domestic product increased 11%</span> to Rs. 9.16 trillion (the money value of all final goods and services in an economy).

## 3. Sri Lanka launched a five‑year export plan targeting US$ 36 billion by 2030.

The roadmap set priorities across <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">eight sectors</span>, from digital services and electronics to boat building and processed foods. Cross‑cutting actions included trade facilitation, standards, skills and logistics, developed with technical support from the <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">Asian Development Bank</span> (a multilateral development bank that lends and advises in Asia).

Officials said the aim was to <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">broaden the basket</span> and deepen value‑chain participation, moving beyond promotion to tackle bottlenecks. The programme aligns with fiscal reforms and seeks <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">non‑debt foreign exchange</span> to strengthen reserve buffers.

## 4. A nationwide dengue drive intensified as admissions strained hospitals.

Authorities focused inspections on schools, factories, offices and homes during a <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">designated control week</span>. Health leaders warned of overcrowding and reported breeding sites at <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">41% of inspected schools</span>, 28% of religious places and 26% of industrial locations.

Campaigns prioritised <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">source reduction</span>, with <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">clean‑up operations</span>, enforcement and public messaging. Planners cautioned that timely destruction of breeding grounds is the most effective short‑term relief for case loads.

## 5. A senior officials’ committee was appointed to study a unified, tripartite governance model for EPF and ETF.

The review will assess feasibility of integrating oversight with a board representing government, employers and employees (a <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">tripartite structure</span> that shares decision‑making among three constituencies). Combined member assets <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">exceed Rs. 5.5 trillion</span> across the two funds.

Analysts said aligning with <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">International Labour Organisation</span> principles could strengthen safeguards and transparency. Any changes would require <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">legislative amendments</span> and operational transition plans.

## 6. A court ordered the arrest of a former minister in an alleged 2014 public‑funds case.

Magistrates directed production before court in a matter concerning <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">Rs. 7.8 million</span> reportedly spent on T‑shirts during a provincial poll. Investigators said charges were filed under the <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">Public Property Act</span> (a statute that criminalises misappropriation of state assets).

Another suspect received bail with a <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">travel ban</span>, while further action awaits guidance from the <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">Attorney General</span>.

## 7. A bank said its takeover of a multinational’s retail and wealth business would complete by 31 July.

The acquirer disclosed the timeline in a filing after obtaining prior approvals. The transaction follows a regional exit by the seller and is expected to bolster <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">affluent and consumer segments</span>.

Market watchers said integration would add scale ahead of a gradual <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">credit upturn</span>. A separate lender opened a Rs. 10–15 billion debenture issue to strengthen <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">Tier 2 capital buffers</span>.

## 8. Tea production in May fell 2.1% year‑on‑year to 24.91 million kilogrammes.

High‑ and medium‑grown output contracted, while low‑grown volumes rose slightly to <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">14.51 million kilogrammes</span>. Cumulative January–May production <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">declined 4.5%</span> to 108.95 million kilogrammes compared with a year earlier.

Compared with 2024, the five‑month total was <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">higher by 4.15 million kilogrammes</span> as weather and field conditions improved from a lower base.

## 9. The airport launched a Rs. 15 billion project to upgrade an ageing terminal.

Plans include expanded departures, redesigned arrivals and modernised baggage systems to ease congestion. Officials said overall capacity would rise to about <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">15 million passengers</span> annually once works finish.

The interim upgrade bridges demand until a <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">Japan‑assisted</span> new terminal is delivered. Funding was <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">arranged domestically</span> to accelerate timelines.

## 10. Partial services on the upcountry railway were set to resume after cyclone damage repairs.

Engineers completed works between key hill‑country stations, enabling operations from the weekend under a <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">special timetable</span>. Additional stretches remain under <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">rehabilitation</span> after landslides and washouts.

Authorities said further sections would <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">reopen in phases</span> as <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">stability testing</span> concludes. Freight and long‑distance links are being prioritised alongside commuter services.

## 11. The road‑safety regulator fixed a date to strictly enforce seat belts on expressways for all occupants.

Officials ended the grace period and confirmed fines would apply <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">from 20 June</span>. <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">Rear‑seat compliance is mandatory</span> on high‑speed corridors, while front‑seat rules continue on other roads.

Police cited recent collisions where belted occupants suffered <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">fewer injuries</span>. Campaigns are targeting retrofitting and <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">public awareness</span> to lift usage rates.
